zh.Dartmouth, Devon, England: 英國英格蘭戴文達特姆斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Swete Wemund  Austyn於大約1304年出生在英國英格蘭戴文達特姆斯時，他的父親Robert Swete27歲，母親Mary Periam23歲。他於大約1346年與Matilda Folkroy在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世在英國。

zh-hans.Dartmouth, Devon, England: 英国英格兰戴文達特姆斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Swete Wemund  Austyn于大约1304年出生在英国英格兰戴文達特姆斯时，他的父亲Robert Swete27岁，母亲Mary Periam23岁。他于大约1346年与Matilda Folkroy在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Swete
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Czech, Slovak, and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Švec ‘shoemaker’ (see Svec ), and of its Ukrainian, Rusyn, Belorussian, and Jewish cognate Shvets .

variants.name: Shvets
meanings: Ukrainian, Rusyn, Belorussian, and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): occupational name for shoemaker, from Ukrainian, Rusyn, and Belorussian shvets. Compare Swetz .

variants.name: Svec
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Czech and Slovak Milan, Frantisek, Jaroslav, Lubos, Vaclav.
Czech, Slovak, and Rusyn (from Slovakia) (Švec): occupational name for a shoemaker, from švec, a derivative of Czech šíti, Slovak šiť ‘to sew’. Compare Schwetz , Shvets , and Swetz .

nameMeanings.name: Wemund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Swiss German: cognate of French Raymond and German Raimund.

variants.name: Raymond
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Andre, Camille, Jacques, Lucien, Armand, Francois, Emile, Fernand, Gilles, Laurier, Marcel.
English and French: from the Norman personal name Raimund or Raimond, from ancient Germanic Raginmund, composed of the elements ragin ‘advice, counsel’ + mund ‘protection’. Compare Ramo and Raymo .
Americanized form of Italian Raimondo and German Raimund, cognates of 1 above. Compare Raimond .

nameMeanings.name: Austyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, and German: from the personal name Austin, from Latin Augustinus, a derivative of Augustus (see Augustin ). This was an extremely common personal name in every part of Western Europe during the Middle Ages, owing its popularity chiefly to Saint Augustine of Hippo (354–430), whose influence on Christianity is generally considered to be second only to that of Saint Paul. Various religious orders came to be formed following rules named in his honor, including the ‘Austin canons’, established in the 11th century, and the ‘Austin friars’, a mendicant order dating from the 13th century. The popularity of the personal name in England was further increased by the fact that it was borne by Saint Augustine of Canterbury (died c. 605), an Italian Benedictine monk known as ‘the Apostle of the English’, who brought Christianity to southern England in 597 and founded the see of Canterbury.
English: variant of   Aspden  , with which this surname became confused.

History: This was the name of a merchant family that became established in eastern MA in the 17th century, notably in Charlestown. Richard Austin came from England and landed at Boston in 1638, and his son Anthony was clerk of Suffield, CT, in 1674. The surname is very common in England as well as America; this Richard Austin was only one of a number of bearers who brought it to North America. — In 1821 Stephen F. Austin (1793–1836), born in Austinville VA, founded the first Anglo colony in TX.

variants.name: Augustine
meanings: Americanized form of any of various European surnames from personal names derived from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ), for example German, Dutch, French, and Czech Augustin 1, Dutch Augustijn, Polish Augustyn , Slovenian Avguštin or Auguštin (see Augustin 2), Italian Agostino .
History: This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Agostino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Salvatore, Antonio, Nicola, Vincenzo, Carlo, Carmelo, Cono, Cosmo, Domenico, Elio, Enzo, Lino, Armando, Arturo, Atilio, Octavio, Silvana.
Italian: from the personal name Agostino, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ).

variants.name: Agoston
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Tibor, Attila, Csaba, Gabor, Geza, Istvan, Jeno, Laszlo.
Hungarian (Ágoston): from the personal name Ágoston, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ). This surname is also common in Romania.

variants.name: Auston
meanings: English:
variant of   Austin  .
variant of   Owston  .

variants.name: Augenstein
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Manfred.
German: from an altered form of the personal name Augustin (see Austin ).

variants.name: Augustin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Andre, Fernand, Jacques, Jean Claude, Monique, Renold, Andree, Antoine, Arsene, Celine, Dany.  German Kurt, Fritz, Gunter, Hans, Erwin, Georg, Gerda, Inge, Juergen, Klaus, Matthias, Otto.

French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, and Czech; Slovak (mainly Augustín); Croatian (also Auguštin): from the personal name Augustin, Slovak Augustín, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ). In North America, this surname may also be a shortened form of the patronymics and other derivatives of these personal names, such as Slovak Augustiňák. This surname is by far most common in Haiti.

Americanized form of Slovenian Avguštin or Auguštin: from the personal name Avguštin, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ). In North America, this surname may also be a shortened and altered form of the Slovenian patronymics like Avguštinčič. Compare Augustine .

variants.name: Agustin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Conrado, Ernesto, Renato, Rodolfo, Salvador, Antonio, Carlos, Francisco, Manuel, Adela, Alfredo, Alicia.
Spanish (Agustín): from the personal name Agustín, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ).

variants.name: Augustus
meanings: Dutch, German, English, Welsh, West Indian (mainly Trinidad and Tobago), and African (mainly Nigeria): from the Latin personal name Augustus (from the adjective augustus ‘consecrated, majestic, venerable’), or a Latinized (humanistic) form of German and English August . It was the Emperor Augustus (63 BC – AD 14), after whom it became conventional for Roman emperors to adopt Augustus as a title on their accession and in whose honor the month of August was named. The personal name became popular among early Christians, who read into it the implication that the bearer had become greater by being baptized. The surname Augustus is virtually non-existing in Germany.

variants.name: Austen
meanings: English: variant of   Austin  , associated chiefly with southeastern England, especially Kent.
German: from a shortened form of the personal name   Augustin  .

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Swete Wemund  Austyn was born about 1304, in Dartmouth, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert Swete, was 27 and his mother, Mary Periam, was 23. He married Matilda Folkroy about 1346, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Swete Wemund  Austyn est né environ 1304, à Dartmouth, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Swete, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Mary Periam, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Matilda Folkroy environ 1346, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Swete Wemund  Austyn um 1304 in Dartmouth, Devon, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Swete 27 und seine Mutter Mary Periam 23. Er hat um 1346 in England Matilda Folkroy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Swete Wemund  Austyn è nato il circa 1304 nel Dartmouth, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Robert Swete, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Mary Periam, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Matilda Folkroy circa 1346, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel England.

ja.Dartmouth, Devon, England: イギリスイギリスデボンダルトモウス
ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Swete Wemund  Austynが約1304年にイギリスイギリスデボンダルトモウスで生まれた時，父Robert Sweteは27歳，母Mary Periamは23歳であった。約1346年にイギリスでMatilda Folkroyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼はイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Swete Wemund  Austyn(이)가 대략 1304년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 더르트모우스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Swete(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Periam(은)는 23세였다. 그는 대략 1346년 영국에서 Matilda Folkroy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 영국에서 사망했다.

mn.Dartmouth, Devon, England: Dartmouth, Devon, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Swete Wemund  Austyn nasceu aproximadamente 1304, em Dartmouth, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Robert Swete, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Mary Periam, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Matilda Folkroy aproximadamente 1346, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Swete Wemund  Austyn родился около 1304 г. в местности дартмоут, девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert Swete было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Periam, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda Folkroy около 1346 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Swete Wemund  Austyn nació aproximadamente 1304, en Dartmouth, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Robert Swete, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Mary Periam, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Matilda Folkroy aproximadamente 1346, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en Inglaterra.
